ELPAC Level 1
Lexile BR–300L
DTR · TALK ABOUT A SCENE

Every Letter Has a Shape

STS-MS7-L1-BR300-MSELA7-PPX-NAV-01 • MS ELA Grade 7 • Analyze the Structure an Author Uses to Organize a Text
Look at the picture. You will answer some questions about it. Press the play button to hear each question. Record your answer for each question. You can listen to your answer and record it again. Click SUBMIT RECORDINGS when you have answered all the questions.
JUST FOR FUN · OPTIONAL
June's class trades letters with pen pals in Montana, Quebec, and Mexico City. Every letter that arrives is organized a different way. Dakota's pen pal told her whole story in time order, from the first snow of winter to the last. June's pen pal wrote about a problem in his town and then explained how his neighbors solved it.
If you wrote to a pen pal far away, would you organize your letter in time order or as a problem and a solution? Why?
Optional. You do not have to record this one.
1
What is June doing?
Look at the picture. Say: June is ____. (writing • reading)
2
What is Dakota holding?
Look at the picture. Say: Dakota is holding ____. (a letter • a book)
3
What is the teacher doing?
Look at the picture. Say: The teacher is ____. (touching the map • opening a door)
4
What place is this? How do you know?
Look at the picture. Say: This is a ____. Then say: I see ____. (library • gym) (books • balls)
5
Tell me about the chart on the wall.
Look at the picture. Say: The chart has three ____. (boxes • doors)
6
What is Cody wearing?
Look at the picture. Say: Cody is wearing ____. (a red hoodie • a blue cap)
Record an answer for each question, then submit.
Content alignment: CCSS RI.7.5 — Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text. ELD (Speaking — official ELPAC “Talk About a Scene”): PI.A.1, PII.B.3, PII.B.4, PII.B.5.
